sections.paragraph:
γνωστός, ή, όν (Aeschyl. et al.; Pla., X.; SIG 800, 34; LXX; En 98:12; PsSol 14:8; ApcSed).

① pert. to being familiar or known: known (the usual mng. in LXX)

ⓐ of things: a remarkable miracle Ac 4:16. γνωστόν ἐστί τινι it is known to someone 2:14; εἰς τὸ γ. εἶναι πᾶσιν that it might be known to all 1 Cl 11:2. W. ὅτι foll. (En 98:12) Ac 4:10; 13:38; 28:28 (MWilcox, The Semitisms of Ac, ’65, 90f). περὶ τῆς αἱρέσεως ταύτης γ. ἡμῖν ἐστιν concerning this sect it is known to us 28:22. γνωστόν τινι ποιεῖν τι make someth. known to someone Hs 5, 5, 1. γνωστὸν γίνεσθαί τινι become known to someone Ac 1:19; 19:17. Abs. 9:42.—κύριος (ὁ) ποιῶν ταῦτα γ. ἀπʼ αἰῶνος the Lord who makes this known from of old 15:17f.

ⓑ of pers.: γ. w. gen. or dat., subst. acquaintance, friend, intimate (4 Km 10:11; 2 Esdr 15:10; Ps 30:12; 54:14; 87:9, 19) J 18:15f; pl. (SibOr 1, 76) Lk 23:49. Abs. in 2:44.

② pert. to being able to be known, capable of being known, intelligible (Pla.; Epict. 2, 20, 4; Gen 2:9; also prob. Sir 21:7; PLaur III, 60, 17 [restored]; Hippol., Ref. 6, 24, 4) γνωστὰ αὐτῷ γίνονται τὰ ῥήματα the words become intelligible to him Hs 5, 4, 3; τὸ γ. (PAmh 145, 9): τὸ γνωστὸν τοῦ θεοῦ what can be known about God or God, to the extent that he can be known (cp. Philo, Leg. All. 1, 60f) Ro 1:19 (s. PSchjött, ZNW 4, 1903, 75–78; AKlöpper, ZWT 47, 1904, 169–80; HDaxer, Ro 1:18–2:10, diss. Rostock 1914, 4ff; AFridrichsen, ZNW 17, 1916, 159–68).—DELG s.v. γιγνώσκω p. 224. M-M. New Docs 4, 143. TW.

Aeschyl Aeschyl , V b.c.—List 5

et al. et al. = et alii (and others)

Pla Pla , V–IV b.c.; s. also Ps.-Plato—List 5

  1. X. = Xenophon, V–IV b.c.—List 5

SIG SIG = Sylloge Inscriptionum Graecarum3; superscript omitted in text—List 3

LXX LXX = Septuaginta, ed. ARahlfs, unless otherwise specified—Lists 2, beg.

En En = I Enoch—List 2

PsSol PsSol = Psalms of Solomon—List 2

ApcSed ApcSed = Apocalypse of Sedrach—List 2

pert. pert. = pertaining (to)

mng. mng. = meaning(s)

1 Cl 1 Cl = 1 Clement—List 1

foll. foll. = followed, following

someth. someth. = something

Hs Hs = Similitudes

pers. pers. = person(s)

gen. gen. = genitive

dat. dat. = dative

subst. subst. = substantive(ly)

  1. pl. = plural

SibOr SibOr = Oracula Sibyllina, II–III a.d.—List 5

Epict Epict , various works, I–II a.d.—List 5

prob. prob. = probable, probably

PLaur PLaur = Dai Papiri della Biblioteca Medicea Laurenziana—List 4

Hippol Hippol , II–III a.d.—List 5

PAmh PAmh = The Amherst Papyri I–II—List 4

  1. cp. = compare, freq. in ref. to citation fr. ancient texts

Philo Philo = P. of Alexandria, I b.c.–I a.d.—List 5

ZNW ZNW = Zeitschrift für die ntl. Wissenschaft—List 6

ZWT ZWT = Zeitschrift für Wissenschaftliche Theologie—List 6

diss. diss. = dissertation

DELG DELG = PChantraine, Dictionnaire étymologique de la langue grecque—List 6

s.v. s.v. = sub voce (under the word, look up the word)

M-M M-M = JMoulton/GMilligan, Vocabulary of Greek Testament—Lists 4, 6

TW TW = Theologisches Wörterbuch zum NT; tr. GBromiley, Theological Dictionary of the NT—List 6

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 204
